Detection mode project - Analysis

Author

Martina Brayley

Published

August 19, 2024

Version 3 - including missing as a category in regression models for TC only - risk factors complete case

Note

Agreed on 29/07/2024 to include missing in the model as a separate category

1 Set up

The Master script links all parts of data preparation in 7 processing scripts. The code below excecutes the master file, running all 7 scripts within. The final product is the analytical dataset called an_df which combined all required variables from casummary, risk factors, mode of detection and mammographic density.

Data summary - commented out as output too large

#skim(an_df)
#dfSummary(an_df)

2 Preparation

Create lists of model variables as objects in the environment for easier manipulation.

Relevel reference groups within some exploratory variables.

Mammographic density is a special variable that will be used for subanalyses on cases with mammographic density only (to adjust for in the model)

Note

On 13/06/2024 discussed with Amy that it is probably better to use alchol units in the analyses instead of alcohol status or both. Looking back at L’s tables that seems to be the same as what was done originally.

2.0.1 Create a dataset with only the required variables

df <- an_df %>% 
  select(d_dmode_n, d_dmode, d_md_avail_lab, d_md_avail, d_parous_lab, d_md, d_md_qrt, any_of(all_expl_variables), any_of(all_expl_variables_tr), d_inv_status_lab, any_of(tumour_char), any_of(tumour_char_tr), any_of(time_vars)
         )
names(df)

2.0.2 Notes on missing values

  1. Original approach (available in analysis.qmd)

There are no missing values in outcome as the analytical dataset was prepared by selecting cases with mode of detection which had to be either SD or I.

In v3 of script 7 error codes were recoded to NA as it is automatically handled in analysis. This dataset should be used. If error codes are of interest, rerun v2 of script 7 in master file, which will produce a dataset with error codes. The following code was written for data processed by v3 of script 7 where missing values are NA.

Even though NAs are automatically handled in glm(), to have better control over which rows are dropped and for consistency, they will be handled manually by the following steps for each model:

  • select only the required variables for the specific model

  • drop NAs using drop_na() function for all exposures and covariates in the model

The same steps will be then used to produce descriptive table (for publication Table 3) which will be merged with logistic regression results. Therefore, it is important that the number of observations are the same in the descriptive and analytical part of the table.

  1. Updated approach (this script - analysis_v2(with_missing).qmd)

This approach has been agreed with Montse on 29/07/2024

There are no missing values in outcome as the analytical dataset was prepared by selecting cases with mode of detection which had to be either SD or I.

In v3 of script 7 error codes were recoded to NA not differentiating between different reasons of missing data (e.g. quation not answered, questionnaire not returned, error in value etc). This dataset is used for the updated analysis. If error codes are of interest, rerun v2 of script 7 in master file, which will produce a dataset with error codes. The following code was written for data processed by v4 of script 7 where missing values are NA.

  1. analysis_v3 - combination of approaches

NA’s in risk factors have only a small number of observations in the missing category which causes errors in regression models. Therefore risk factors will be processed according to the original approach.

Tumour characteristics have a lot of missing values, mostly caused by PR status. Therefore in TC analysis the missing will be processed as a separate category in regression.

2.0.2.1 Recode all missing values in analytical variables

vis_dat(df)
#vis_miss(df)

gg_miss_var(df)
# an_df %>% 
#   map(pct_miss)


# convert all factor variables to character 
df <- df %>% 
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char), as.character)) %>% 
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char_tr), as.character))


# convert all character variables NAs to "Missing" 
df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char), ~ replace_na(., "Missing"))) %>% 
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char_tr), ~ replace_na(., "Missing")))

#str(df)

#vis_dat(df) # only missing are those associated with MD which is a subset of the data

# convert all character variables back to factor
df <- df %>%
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char), as.factor)
         ) %>% 
  mutate(across(any_of(tumour_char_tr), as.factor))


#str(df)

vis_dat(df)
# %>%
#   mutate(across(
#     any_of(tumour_char), 
#     ~ fct_expand(., "Missing") %>% fct_relevel("Missing", after = Inf))
#     )

#freq(df)
df %>% tabyl(d_dmode_n)
d_dmode_nnpercent
011850.611
17550.389
# remove rows with missing error codes in specified columns

# data <- an_df %>%
#   filter(!if_any(all_of(all_expl_variables), ~ . %in% error_codes))



# check numbers and percentages of missing values (coded out as output is too long)

# data %>% 
#   map(pct_miss)
# 
# data %>% 
#   map(n_miss)

2.0.3 Relevel required categorical variables

Change the reference groups in the following categorical variables:

  • BMI: reference = normal weight

  • hormone receptor status (ER, PR) and HER2: reference = positive

BMI amended in this script directly before analysis as for descriptives the original level ordering will be needed

Also, relevel variables where “Missing” level is not last (levels jumped when converteting NA to “Missing”

(smoking, hrt, hrt - trick, oc, pre-menopausal status)

df <- df %>% 
  mutate(d_bmi_entry_lab = fct_relevel(d_bmi_entry_lab, "18.5-25", "<18.5", "25-30", "30+"),
         d_pos_nodes_lab = fct_relevel(d_pos_nodes_lab, "Negative", "Positive", "Missing"),
         d_er_status_lab = fct_relevel(d_er_status_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
         d_pr_status_lab = fct_relevel(d_pr_status_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
         d_her2_status_lab = fct_relevel(d_her2_status_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
         d_pos_nodes_tr_lab = fct_relevel(d_pos_nodes_tr_lab, "Negative", "Positive", "Missing"),
         d_er_tr_lab = fct_relevel(d_er_tr_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
         d_pr_tr_lab = fct_relevel(d_pr_tr_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
         d_her2_tr_lab = fct_relevel(d_her2_tr_lab, "Positive", "Negative", "Missing"),
  )

#freq(df)


saveRDS(df, file = "Q:/SHARED/USERS/MBrayley/Screening/data/df_from_analysis_script.rds")

3 Univariate analysis

Repeat these steps for each exposure:

  1. Tabulation including missing
  2. Compute basic OR and chisqr test
  3. Univariate logistic regression
Important

Code under the empty heading has been excluded from the html file as not relevant

3.1 Define function for univariate analysis

3.2 Compute univariate analysis

3.2.1 All cases

3.2.2 Cases with Breast density

3.3 Univariate analysis tables

Note

Comparing all cases vs cases with MD only

# Univariate analysis with gtsummary

# all cases:
univ1 <- df %>%
  select(d_dmode_n, any_of(all_expl_variables)
         ) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = glm,
    y = d_dmode_n,
    method.args = list(family = binomial),
    exponentiate = TRUE,
    pvalue_fun = ~ style_pvalue(.x, digits = 2)
   ) %>%
  add_global_p() %>% # add global p-value
  add_nevent() %>% # add number of events of the outcome
  bold_labels() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

#univ1

 
# show_header_names(t1) # show header names to rename event N to Interval N


# MD cases only:
univ2 <- df %>%
  filter(d_md_avail == 1
  ) %>%
  select(d_dmode_n, any_of(all_expl_variables)
         ) %>%
  tbl_uvregression(
    method = glm,
    y = d_dmode_n,
    method.args = list(family = binomial),
    exponentiate = TRUE,
    pvalue_fun = ~ style_pvalue(.x, digits = 2)
   ) %>%
  add_global_p() %>% # add global p-value
  add_nevent() %>% # add number of events of the outcome
  bold_labels() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")


# merge tables --------------------------------------------------------------- 


#univariate <- tbl_merge(tbls = list(t1, t2))


univariate <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(univ1, univ2),
  tab_spanner = c("**All cases**", "**Cases with MD**")
   ) 

univariate

4 Test trick models

There are 3 groups of variables that require trick recoding (Tumour characteristics, parity related and menopause related). These variables are closely related and have 1 group within them that is the same in all of the variables. For example, if we look at the parity variables (binary parous, n of parous pregnancies, age at first birth), all 3 variables have a level of “non-parous” women, which all cannot be in the model. Therefore, to trick the model we recode the non-parous women in n of parous preg and age at first birth variables to the reference group. Although, it may seem counter intuitive, when the equation is written down including the indicator variables, the maths works.

When we run 2 models:

  1. a model on a subset of parous women only and with the remaining 2 parity related variables
  2. a model with all cases, the parous binary variable and the 2 recoded parity related variables (non parous women part of the reference group)

The estimates will be exactly the same. This needs to be tested for all 3 groups of these variables.

17/06/2024

Agreed with Amy and Montse to remove the binary parity variable from the model and only use n of pregnancied. The reference group will be 0 children.

4.1 Parity related variables

# tabulate trick vars by parity to see if recoded properly -----------------------

## age at birth
df %>% tabyl(d_agebirth1_tr_lab, d_parity_lab) %>% 
  adorn_title()
d_parity_lab

0034

  adorn_title()

df %>%  tabyl(d_parous_lab, d_parity_lab)
d_parous_lab0123+

Parous02111.04e+03433
## number of parous pregnancies
# df %>% tabyl(d_parity_tr_lab, d_parous_lab) %>% 
#   adorn_title()
# df %>% tabyl(d_parity_lab, d_parous_lab) %>% 
#   adorn_title()

# MODEL A - subset on parous women ------------------------------------------------

model <- df %>% 
 filter(d_parity_lab != "0") %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_agebirth1_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))
#summary(model)
#logistic.display(model)

tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level") %>% 
  add_nevent() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
Characteristic Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value
d_agebirth1_lab
656


    <20 80
— —
    20-24 505
0.82 0.51, 1.33 0.4
    25-29 677
0.78 0.49, 1.26 0.3
    30-34 296
0.90 0.55, 1.49 0.7
    35+ 117
0.83 0.47, 1.49 0.5
1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval 
# MODEL B - binary parity status in the model -------------------------------------

model <- df %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~  d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))
#summary(model)
#logistic.display(model)

tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level") %>% 
  add_nevent() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
Characteristic Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value

Note

Trick on parity variables DISCUSS

when binary parous status was in the model the estimates were exact now slightly different.

4.2 Menopause related variables

# tabulate trick vars by parity to see if recoded properly 

## age at menopause
df %>% tabyl(d_age_meno_tr_lab, d_R1menopause_lab3) %>% 
  adorn_title()
d_R1menopause_lab3
d_age_meno_tr_labPremenopausalPostmenopausal
<50597301
50-540902
55+0135
05
## HRT use 
df %>% tabyl(d_R1hrt_tr_lab, d_R1menopause_lab3) %>% 
  adorn_title()
d_R1menopause_lab3
d_R1hrt_tr_labPremenopausalPostmenopausal
Never595546
Former0442
Current0349
26
# MODEL A - subset on postmenopausal  women ---------------------------------
model <- df %>% 
  filter(d_R1menopause_lab3 == "Postmenopausal") %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_age_meno_tr_lab + d_R1hrt_tr_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))
#summary(model)
#logistic.display(model)

tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "label") %>% 
  add_nevent() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
Characteristic Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value

— —
    Former

1.10 0.84, 1.43 0.5
    Current

1.58 1.20, 2.09 0.001

# MODEL B - binary parity status in the model -------------------------------------

model <- df %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab + d_R1hrt_tr_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))
#summary(model)
#logistic.display(model)

tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "label") %>% 
  add_nevent() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

Note

Trick on menopausae variables SUCCESSFULL

4.3 Tumour characteristics variables

All DCIS, including those with missing values in the variables below were coded as the reference group. This approach was done also by Louise. Assuming most DCIS would be missing so this seems correct.

# note: keeping morhology as categorical without labels as it affects the outpu

model <- df %>% 
  filter(d_inv_status == 1) %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_grade_lab + d_morph4_lab + d_pos_nodes_lab + d_tumour_size_lab + d_er_status_lab + d_pr_status_lab + d_her2_status_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

  add_n(location = "level") %>% 
  add_nevent() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

Note

Trick on Tumour characteristics variables SUCCESSFULL

4.4 Conclusion of trick test

Trick test on all 3 sets of variables was successful. This means that we can use the trick variables in the multivariate analysis.

5 Multivariate analysis

This is for the main analysis, subanalyses will be an independent section

The main analysis is comprised of 2 parts:

  1. Examining the relationship between risk factors and mode of detection
  2. Adjusting for mammographic density in a subset of cases with density available

5.1 Part 1: Relationship between risk factors and mode of detection

An analysis with all selected exposures in the model (adjusted for each other). Also, will need to adjust for time and age variables. Then, adjust for tumour characteristics and explore the difference. If there is no or minimal difference the model adjusted for tumour characteristics will be presented in the supplement.

Therefore, part 1 of the multivariate analysis will include 3 models:

  1. Minimally adjusted - including all risk factor exposures
  2. Adjusted for time variables
  3. Fully adjusted - time variables and tumour characteristics.

Model 1: “d_R1alcohol_status_cat”, “d_R1alcohol_units_cat”, “d_R1smokingstatus_cat”, “d_bmi_entry_cat”, “d_R1physmet_leis_who”, “d_bbd”, “d_fambrca”, “d_age_menarche_cat”, “d_ocstatus_cat”, “d_R1menopause_cat3”, “d_age_menopause_cat”, “d_R1hrtstatus_cat”, “d_parous_cat”, “d_parity_cat”, “d_agebirth1_cat”

Model 2: Model 1 + “diagage”, “d_R1toBC_y”

Model 3: subset of those with TC! Model 1 + “diagage”, “d_R1toBC_y”,

Model 4: Model 3 + “d_inv_status”, “d_grade_cat”, “d_morph4”, “d_pos_nodes_cat”, “d_tumour_size”, “d_er_status”, “d_pr_status”, “d_her2_status”

5.1.1 Code

# Model 1 -----------------------------------------------------------
# include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

model <- df %>%
  tidyr::drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y # excluding missing also from covariates as these are included in model 2  (to be comparable with model 2)
  ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
#logistic.display(model)

# # display result as a tidy model table with OR  
#  tidy_model <- model %>% 
#    tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#    mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
#    select(-estimate) %>% 
#    select(term, OR, everything())
#  tidy_model 

model1 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
 
 # model1
 
 # Model 2 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

model <- df %>%
   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y # excluding missing also from covariates as these are included in model 2  (to be comparable with model 2)
  ) %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

 

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# display result as a tidy model table with OR  
 # tidy_model <- model %>%
 #   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
 #   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
 #   select(-estimate) %>%
 #   select(term, OR, everything())
 # tidy_model

model2 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

#model2

# Model 3  ----------------------------------------------------------------
#same as model 2 but on the subset of those with TC

 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis


# filter missing - change line 2 
 
 model <- df %>%
   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# display result as a tidy model table with OR
# tidy_model <- model %>%
#   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
#   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
#   select(-estimate) %>%
#   select(term, OR, everything())
# tidy_model
 
model3 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

#model3

# Model 4 ---------------------------------------------------------------------

# Adding TC trick variables

# filter missing - change line 2
model <- df %>%
   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y
      + d_inv_status + d_grade_tr + d_morph4_tr_lab + d_pos_nodes_tr_lab 
      + d_tmsize_tr_lab + d_er_tr_lab + d_pr_tr_lab + d_her2_tr_lab, 
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))


# # find the problematic variable - adding 1 by 1
# model <- df %>%
#    tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
#            ) %>% 
#   glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
#       + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
#       + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
#       + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
#       + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab + diagage + d_R1toBC_y
#       + d_inv_status, 
#             family = binomial(link = "logit"))
# 
# 
# 
# # try model with only inv_status
# model <- df %>%
#    tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
#            ) %>% 
#   glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_inv_status, 
#             family = binomial(link = "logit"))
# 
# 
# # it drops all 0s (dcis)
# df %>% 
#   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
#                  ) %>% 
#   tabyl(d_dmode_n, d_inv_status)
# 
# # find out where the 0 in inv_status are dropping
# df %>% 
#   tabyl(d_dmode_n, d_inv_status)
# 
# # adding tumour variables to drop_na 1 by 1
# df %>% 
#   tidyr::drop_na(d_inv_status, d_grade_tr, d_morph4_tr_lab, d_pos_nodes_tr_lab, d_tmsize_tr_lab, d_er_tr_lab, d_pr_tr_lab, d_her2_tr_lab) %>% 
#   tabyl(d_dmode_n, d_inv_status)
# 
# 


# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)


#display result as a tidy model table with OR
# tidy_model <- model %>%
#   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
#   mutate(OR = round(estimate, 2),
#          std.error = round(std.error, 2),
#          p.value = round(p.value, 4),
#          conf.low = round(conf.low)
#          ) %>%
#   select(-estimate) %>%
#   select(term, OR, everything())
# tidy_model

model4 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

#model4

on 17/06 we’ve agreed to count all TC missing as missing in the trick model. Previously, if the missing were DCIS (e.g. dcis with missing ER status) they were recoded as the reference group (ER positive). However, when running the model with all original missing as missing in the trick variable with tumour characteristics (e.g. in the model adjusting for tumour characteristics) the number of DCIS was reduced to n = 1 as most missing in tumour variables are DCIS cases. Therefore, the model collapsed.

5.1.2 Tables

These tables are for internal purposes only.

  • All cases
  • Cases with TC

Minimally adjusted vs time and age adjusted

Model 1 - Minimally adjusted - only for other exposures

Model 2 - adjusted for time from entry to BC and age at diagnosis

# create table for model 1 and 2 

model1and2 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(model1, model2),
  tab_spanner = c("**Minimally adjusted**", "**Time and age adjusted**")
   )

d_R1toBC_y




1,707 674 1.06 1.03, 1.10 <0.001
1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
Summary

No changes in estimates after adjusting for age at diagnosis and time from entry to diagnosis

Adjusting for tumour characteristics

Model 3 - model 2 (adjusted model) on subset of cases with TC

Model 4 - adjusting for TC

Model 3 and 4 have the same number of observations n = 857. After adjusting for tumour characteristics, there are some changes in the estimates (e.g. BMI - underweight group although CI large, BBD larger effect)

Note 19/08/2024: this is now all cases and adjusting for TC variables that include missing category.

model3and4 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(model3, model4),
  tab_spanner = c("**Time and age adjusted**", "**Adjusted for TC**")
   ) 

model3and4

1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
Summary

Relatively large changes in estimates for

  • BMI category <18.5kg/m2 (underweight), although the confidence interval is very wide.

  • Benign breast disease - stronger effect after adjusting for tumour characteristics.

5.2 Part 2: Adjusting for mammographic density

This part will require the same analysis as in part 1 on a subset of cases with mamographic density. Then expanding by a 4th model adjusting for mammographic density.

Not modelling TC here as the sample size would be significantly reduced.

Model 5: “d_R1alcohol_status_cat”, “d_R1alcohol_units_cat”, “d_R1smokingstatus_cat”, “d_bmi_entry_cat”, “d_R1physmet_leis_who”, “d_bbd”, “d_fambrca”, “d_age_menarche_cat”, “d_ocstatus_cat”, “d_R1menopause_cat3”, “d_age_menopause_cat”, “d_R1hrtstatus_cat”, “d_parous_cat”, “d_parity_cat”, “d_agebirth1_cat”

Model 6: Model 4 + “diagage”, “d_R1toBC_y”

Model 7: Model 5 + “d_R1toMD_y”, “d_MDtoBC_y” [ decide whether to include all of these]

Model 8: Model 6 +“d_md_qrt” [as quartiles]

5.2.1 Code

# Model 5 -----------------------------------------------------------
# include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# relevel bmis so normal weight is the reference 

model <- df %>%
  filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>% 
  drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>%  
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# display result as a tidy model table with OR  
 # tidy_model <- model %>% 
 #   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
 #   mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
 #   select(-estimate) %>% 
 #   select(term, OR, everything())
 # tidy_model 

model5 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

 
 
 # Model 6 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
   drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# # display result as a tidy model table with OR  
#  tidy_model <- model %>% 
#    tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#    mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
#    select(-estimate) %>% 
#    select(term, OR, everything())
#  tidy_model 

model6 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
 # Model 7 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" and d_MDtoBC_y - not adjusted for R1toMD yet - discuss if needed

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
   drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y + d_MDtoBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# # display result as a tidy model table with OR  
#  tidy_model <- model %>% 
#    tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#    mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
#    select(-estimate) %>% 
#    select(term, OR, everything())
#  tidy_model

model7 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")
 
# Model 8 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y + d_MDtoBC_y
      + d_md_qrt,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# # display result as a tidy model table with OR  
#  tidy_model <- model %>% 
#    tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#    mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
#    select(-estimate) %>% 
#    select(term, OR, everything())
#  tidy_model

model8 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

5.2.2 Tables

This table is for internal purposes only.

# combine results

model5to8 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(model5, model6, model7, model8),
  tab_spanner = c("**Unadjusted**", "**Adjusted for DG age + entry to DG**", "**Adjusted for MDtoDG time**", "**Adjusted for MDtoDG time + MD**")
   ) 

model5to8

Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value Total N Interval N OR1 95% CI1 p-value

1,046 348 0.95 0.89, 1.01 0.10 1,046 348 0.94 0.88, 1.00 0.037

    1

1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
Summary

No significant changes after adjusting for mammographic density.

5.2.3 Comparing adjustment with cont vas cat MD

# Model 8b - with continuous MD ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y + d_MDtoBC_y
      + d_md,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)

# # display result as a tidy model table with OR  
#  tidy_model <- model %>% 
#    tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>% 
#    mutate(OR = estimate) %>% 
#    select(-estimate) %>% 
#    select(term, OR, everything())
#  tidy_model

model8b <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

Comparing model 6, model 8 adjusted for md quartiles and model 8b adjusted for continuous md:

model 6 is a default model, which means it is the final selected model from part 1 adjusted for age at diagnosis and time from entry to diagnosis

model688b <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(model6, model8, model8b),
  tab_spanner = c("**Default**", "**Adjusted MD qrt**", "**Adjusted MD cont**")
   ) 

model688b

5.3 Part 3: Tumour characteristics only

For internal purpose or supplemental materials.

Model 9: TC only “d_inv_status”, “d_grade_cat”, “d_morph4”, “d_pos_nodes_cat”, “d_tumour_size”, “d_er_status”, “d_pr_status”, “d_her2_status”

Model 10: “d_inv_status”, “d_grade_cat”, “d_morph4”, “d_pos_nodes_cat”, “d_tumour_size”, “d_er_status”, “d_pr_status”, “d_her2_status”, “diagage”, “R1toBC”

5.3.1 Code

# Model 9 ------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Adding TC trick variables
 
model <- df %>%
  drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ 
      d_inv_status + d_grade_tr + d_morph4_tr_lab + d_pos_nodes_tr_lab 
      + d_tmsize_tr_lab + d_er_tr_lab + d_pr_tr_lab + d_her2_tr_lab, 
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)
 

# display result as a tidy model table with OR
 # tidy_model <- model %>%
 #   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
 #   mutate(OR = round(estimate, 2),
 #          std.error = round(std.error, 2),
 #          p.value = round(p.value, 4),
 #          conf.low = round(conf.low)
 #          ) %>%
 #   select(-estimate) %>%
 #   select(term, OR, everything())
 # tidy_model

model9 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

# Model 10 ------------------------------------------------------------------------

# adjusting for time from entry to dg and diagage
 
model <- df %>%
  drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ 
      diagage + d_R1toBC_y
      + d_inv_status + d_grade_tr + d_morph4_tr_lab + d_pos_nodes_tr_lab 
      + d_tmsize_tr_lab + d_er_tr_lab + d_pr_tr_lab + d_her2_tr_lab, 
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# summary(model)
# logistic.display(model)
 

# display result as a tidy model table with OR
 # tidy_model <- model %>%
 #   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
 #   mutate(OR = round(estimate, 2),
 #          std.error = round(std.error, 2),
 #          p.value = round(p.value, 4),
 #          conf.low = round(conf.low)
 #          ) %>%
 #   select(-estimate) %>%
 #   select(term, OR, everything())
 # tidy_model

model10 <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>% 
  add_n(location = "level")  %>% 
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")

5.3.2 Table

This table is for internal purpose only.

# combine results 


model9and10 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(model9, model10),
  tab_spanner = c("**Unadjusted**", "**Adjusted for DG age + entry to DG**")
   ) 

model9and10

d_R1toBC_y




1,940 755 1.08 1.05, 1.11 <0.001
1 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval
Summary

Grade 3 of BC, positive nodes, tumour size of 21-50mm associated with being an interval case. Adjusting for age at diagnosis and time from entry to diagnosis did not change the estimates.

6 Draft analysis tables for publication

This section is used for developing tables presenting analyses results for publication.

Table 1 and 2 are descriptive tables, which will be created in a separate Quarto file (Screening descriptives). Once the publication tables are finalised, new Quarto document will be produced with the final tables that can be then exported to different formats.

6.0.1 Table structure:

Main tables

Table 1 - general descriptives (population characteristics)

Table 2 - risk factor descriptives by density (+ figures)

Table 3 - Risk factor descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results (unadjusted for TC) - all cases

Table 4 - subset on cases with MD. Same model as table 3 + adjusted for MD. Include mediation measures (% change)

Supplements

ST 1 - Descriptives for MD cases (same as table 1 but subset)

ST 2 - TC descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results

ST 3 - Risk factor analysis adjusted for tumour characteristics (sensitivity analysis) - on subset of cases with TC

6.1 Table 3 - Main analysis

Model 2 is the final selected model to present in the analysis. Model 2 is adjusted for all co-exposures + age at diagnosis and time from entry to BC diagnosis

# summary Logistic Regression of Model 2  -----------------------------------------

# view existing output
#model2

# remove columns with total and interval N - not simple with gtsummary, trying from scratch without adding the columns


# Create clean version of logistic output, without Ns
model <- df %>%
   drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# tidy up output
model2 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")
  

#model2





# Create descriptives by mode of detection ------------------------------------
# select(d_dmode_n, d_R1alcohol_units_lab, d_R1smokingstatus_lab, d_bmi_entry_lab,  d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab, d_bbd_lab, d_fambrca_lab, d_age_menarche_lab, d_ocstatus_lab, d_R1menopause_lab3, d_age_meno_tr_lab, d_R1hrt_tr_lab, d_parity_tr_lab, d_agebirth1_tr_lab)

# note: need to rename the non-trick variables according to their trick name to align with the model, which can then allow table merge. 

# include missing 
# create df
  
descr_model2_df <- df %>% 
  select(d_dmode, all_of(all_expl_variables), diagage, d_R1toBC_y 
         ) %>% 
  #tidyr::drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables), diagage, d_R1toBC_y # filter out all missing to have the same n as in the model
  mutate(d_bmi_entry_lab = fct_relevel(d_bmi_entry_lab, "<18.5", "18.5-25", "25-30", "30+")
              ) %>% 
  mutate(d_age_meno_tr_lab = d_age_menopause_lab,
         d_R1hrt_tr_lab = d_R1hrtstatus_lab,
         d_agebirth1_tr_lab = d_agebirth1_lab
         ) %>% 
  select(-d_age_menopause_lab, -d_R1hrtstatus_lab, -d_agebirth1_lab, -diagage, -d_R1toBC_y)  
  
  

# glimpse(descr_model2)
# str(test)

# create gs summary table
descr_model2 <- descr_model2_df %>% 
  tbl_summary(by = d_dmode,
              statistic = list(
                all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                all_categorical() ~ "{n}" # add percentage if needed ({p})
                ),
              digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
              label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth"
                ),
              type = list(d_bbd_lab ~ "categorical",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "categorical")
              ) %>% 
  add_overall()

#descr_model2
  
# merge with analysis output -------------------------------------------------


table3 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(descr_model2, model2),
  tab_spanner = c("", "")
   ) 
   
  
   

table3

    30-34 296 122 174 0.81 0.47, 1.42
    35+ 117 46 71 0.69 0.36, 1.33
    Non-parous 258 97 161


    Unknown 7 2 5


1 n
2 OR = Odds Ratio, CI = Confidence Interval 
# this ID is extra in the LR output from model 2 - T276E210
# this is because HRT status was missing as under 50 but it is present in trick hrt as recoded to never. - check what to do with this 



# format the table -------------------------------------------------------------

6.2 Table 4 - adjusting for MD

6.2.0.1 Categorical MD

Table 4 - subset on cases with MD. Same adjustment model as table 3 + adjusted for MD. Include mediation measures (% change) (model 6 and 8)

# Model 6 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
   drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))


# tidy up output
model6 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")

#model6

# create a dataframe with broom::tidy() for forestplots 

tidy_model6 <- model %>%
   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
   select(-estimate) %>%
   select(term, OR, everything())
#tidy_model6


# Model 8 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y + d_MDtoBC_y
      + d_md_qrt,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

model8 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth",
                          d_md_qrt ~ "Mammographic breast density quartile"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y, d_MDtoBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")

#model8

# create a dataframe with broom::tidy() for forestplots 

tidy_model8 <- model %>%
   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
   select(-estimate) %>%
   select(term, OR, everything())
 
# Descriptives for table 4 -------------------------------------------------
# subset on those with mammo density 


descr_MDsubset_df <- df %>% 
  select(d_dmode, all_of(all_expl_variables), diagage, d_R1toBC_y, d_md_qrt 
         ) %>% 
  tidyr::drop_na(d_md_qrt # filter out all missing to have the same n as in the model
  ) %>% 
  mutate(d_bmi_entry_lab = fct_relevel(d_bmi_entry_lab, "<18.5", "18.5-25", "25-30", "30+")
              ) %>% 
  mutate(d_age_meno_tr_lab = d_age_menopause_lab,
         d_R1hrt_tr_lab = d_R1hrtstatus_lab,
         d_agebirth1_tr_lab = d_agebirth1_lab
         ) %>% 
  select(-d_age_menopause_lab, -d_R1hrtstatus_lab, -d_agebirth1_lab, -diagage, -d_R1toBC_y)  
  
  

# glimpse(descr_MDsubset_df)
# str(descr_MDsubset_df)

# create gs summary table
descr_MDsubset <- descr_MDsubset_df %>% 
  tbl_summary(by = d_dmode,
              statistic = list(
                all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                all_categorical() ~ "{n}" # add percentage if needed ({p})
                ),
              digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
              label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth",
                          d_md_qrt ~ "Mammographic breast density quartile"
                ),
              type = list(d_bbd_lab ~ "categorical",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "categorical")
              ) #%>% 
 # add_overall()


#descr_MDsubset



# merge tables -------------------------------------------------


table4 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(descr_MDsubset, model6, model8),
  tab_spanner = c("Subset of cases with MD", "Unadjusted for MD", "Adjusted for MD")
   ) 
   
  
   

#table4

Adding pct difference into the table

#  using gtsummary 
table4_diff <- table4 %>% 
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x %>% 
      mutate(pct_diff = if_else(
          is.na(estimate_2) | is.na(estimate_3),
          "",
          sprintf("%.1f", (estimate_3 - estimate_2) / estimate_3 * 100))
    )
    ) %>% 
  modify_header(
    pct_diff = "**Difference (%)**"
  )

table4_diff

6.2.0.2 Forest plot for table 4 - adjusting for categorical MD

Using tidy_model outputs from model 4 and 6 - these are data frame format.

# full join data from both models 
md_forest_df <- tidy_model6 %>% 
  full_join(tidy_model8, by = "term", suffix = c("_mdl6", "_mdl8"))

# RESUME HERE
# create labels and variable names structures 
md_forest_df <- md_forest_df %>% 
  mutate(variable = case_when(
    grepl("menopause", term) ~ "Menopausal status",
    grepl("age_meno", term) ~ "Age at menopause",
    grepl("hrt", term) ~ "HRT status",
    grepl("parity", term) ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
    grepl("agebirth1", term) ~ "Age at first birth",
    grepl("age_menarche", term) ~ "Age at menarche", 
    grepl("ocstatus", term) ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
    grepl("bbd", term) ~ "Benign breast disease",
    grepl("fambrca", term) ~ "Family history of BC",
    grepl("R1alcohol", term) ~ "Alcohol units",
    grepl("R1smokingstatus", term) ~ "Smoking status",
    grepl("bmi_entry", term) ~ "BMI",
    grepl("R1physmet_leis_who", term) ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
    grepl("md_qrt", term) ~ "Mammographic breast density quartile"
                              ),
    level = case_when(
      grepl("d_R1menopause_lab3", term) ~ sub("d_R1menopause_lab3", "", term),
      grepl("age_meno", term) ~ sub("d_age_meno_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("hrt", term) ~ sub("d_R1hrt_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("parity", term) ~ sub("d_parity_lab", "", term),
      grepl("agebirth1", term) ~ sub("d_agebirth1_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("age_menarche", term) ~ sub("d_age_menarche_lab", "", term), 
      grepl("ocstatus", term) ~ sub("d_ocstatus_lab", "", term),
      grepl("bbd", term) ~ sub("d_bbd_lab", "", term),
      grepl("fambrca", term) ~ sub("d_fambrca_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1alcohol", term) ~ sub("d_R1alcohol_units_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1smokingstatus", term) ~ sub("d_R1smokingstatus_lab", "", term),
      grepl("bmi_entry", term) ~ sub("d_bmi_entry_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1physmet_leis_who", term) ~ sub("d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab", "", term),
      grepl("md_qrt", term) ~ sub("d_md_qrt", "", term)
    )
    )

# add subheading rows 

subheading_data <- md_forest_df %>%
  distinct(variable) %>%
  mutate(level = variable)

md_forest_df <- bind_rows(subheading_data, md_forest_df) %>%
  arrange(variable, factor(level, levels = unique(level)))



# create long format 
md_forest_long <- md_forest_df %>% 
  filter(!term %in% c("(Intercept)", "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y", "d_MDtoBC_y", "d_md_qrt2", "d_md_qrt3", "d_md_qrt4"),
         !is.na(level),
         level != "Mammographic breast density quartile"
         ) %>% 
  select(level, OR_mdl6, conf.low_mdl6, conf.high_mdl6, OR_mdl8, conf.low_mdl8, conf.high_mdl8
         ) %>% 
  mutate(pct_diff = if_else(is.na(OR_mdl6), "-", as.character(round((OR_mdl8 - OR_mdl6) / OR_mdl8 * 100, 1))) 
         ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(OR_mdl6, conf.low_mdl6, conf.high_mdl6, 
             OR_mdl8, conf.low_mdl8, conf.high_mdl8
             ),
    names_to = c(".value", "mdl"),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) 
is_summary <- is.na(md_forest_long$OR)


# develop forest

md_forest_long %>% 
  group_by(mdl
           ) %>% 
  mutate(mean = OR,
         lower = conf.low,
         upper = conf.high
         ) %>% 
  forestplot(labeltext = c(level, pct_diff),
             xlog = FALSE,
             xlab = "OR",
             boxsize = 0.3,
             zero = 1,
             line.margin = 0.5,
             clip = c(0, 3),
             ticks = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3),
             ci.vertices = T,
             ci.vertices.height = 0.2,
             legend = c("Default model", "Adjusted for MD"),
             title = paste("Final model adjusted vs unadjusted for mamographic breast density quartiles"),
             is.summary = is_summary
             ) %>% 
  fp_set_style(box = c("grey50","deeppink2") %>%  lapply(function(x) gpar(fill = x, col = "#666666")),
               default = gpar(vertices = T),
               line = "grey40",
               summary = gpar(col = NA), # Set summary color to NA
               align = "lrrr",
               hrz_lines = gpar(col = NA), # Set line color to NA
               txt_gp = fpTxtGp(label = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    ticks = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    xlab = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                )
               ) %>% 
  fp_decorate_graph(graph.pos = 2
                    ) %>% 
  fp_add_header(level = c("", "Risk factor"),
                pct_diff = c("", "% difference")
                ) %>% 
  fp_insert_row(level = "",
                position = 3,
                mean = NULL,
                lower = NULL,
                upper = NULL,
                is.summary = TRUE,
                boxsize = NA)

6.2.0.3 Continuous MD

# Model 8b - with continuous MD ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   filter(d_md_avail == 1 # include only those with md
         ) %>%
  drop_na(all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y + d_MDtoBC_y
      + d_md,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

model8b <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth",
                          d_md ~ "Mammographic breast density"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y, d_MDtoBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")

#model8b

# create a dataframe with broom::tidy() for forestplots 

tidy_model8b <- model %>%
   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
   select(-estimate) %>%
   select(term, OR, everything())

Table 4 including continuous MD

table4b <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(descr_MDsubset, model6, model8b),
  tab_spanner = c("Subset of cases with MD", "Unadjusted for MD", "Adjusted for cont MD")
   ) 

#table4b   

#  using gtsummary 
table4b_diff <- table4b %>% 
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x %>% 
      mutate(pct_diff = if_else(
          is.na(estimate_2) | is.na(estimate_3),
          "",
          sprintf("%.1f", (estimate_3 - estimate_2) / estimate_3 * 100))
    )
    ) %>% 
  modify_header(
    pct_diff = "**Difference (%)**"
  )

6.2.0.4 Forest plot with continuous MD

# full join data from both models 
md_forest_df <- tidy_model6 %>% 
  full_join(tidy_model8b, by = "term", suffix = c("_mdl6", "_mdl8"))

# RESUME HERE
# create labels and variable names structures 
md_forest_df <- md_forest_df %>% 
  mutate(variable = case_when(
    grepl("menopause", term) ~ "Menopausal status",
    grepl("age_meno", term) ~ "Age at menopause",
    grepl("hrt", term) ~ "HRT status",
    grepl("parity", term) ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
    grepl("agebirth1", term) ~ "Age at first birth",
    grepl("age_menarche", term) ~ "Age at menarche", 
    grepl("ocstatus", term) ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
    grepl("bbd", term) ~ "Benign breast disease",
    grepl("fambrca", term) ~ "Family history of BC",
    grepl("R1alcohol", term) ~ "Alcohol units",
    grepl("R1smokingstatus", term) ~ "Smoking status",
    grepl("bmi_entry", term) ~ "BMI",
    grepl("R1physmet_leis_who", term) ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
    grepl("md", term) ~ "Mammographic breast density"
                              ),
    level = case_when(
      grepl("d_R1menopause_lab3", term) ~ sub("d_R1menopause_lab3", "", term),
      grepl("age_meno", term) ~ sub("d_age_meno_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("hrt", term) ~ sub("d_R1hrt_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("parity", term) ~ sub("d_parity_lab", "", term),
      grepl("agebirth1", term) ~ sub("d_agebirth1_tr_lab", "", term),
      grepl("age_menarche", term) ~ sub("d_age_menarche_lab", "", term), 
      grepl("ocstatus", term) ~ sub("d_ocstatus_lab", "", term),
      grepl("bbd", term) ~ sub("d_bbd_lab", "", term),
      grepl("fambrca", term) ~ sub("d_fambrca_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1alcohol", term) ~ sub("d_R1alcohol_units_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1smokingstatus", term) ~ sub("d_R1smokingstatus_lab", "", term),
      grepl("bmi_entry", term) ~ sub("d_bmi_entry_lab", "", term),
      grepl("R1physmet_leis_who", term) ~ sub("d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab", "", term),
      grepl("md", term) ~ sub("d_md", "", term)
    )
    )

# add subheading rows 

subheading_data <- md_forest_df %>%
  distinct(variable) %>%
  mutate(level = variable)

md_forest_df <- bind_rows(subheading_data, md_forest_df) %>%
  arrange(variable, factor(level, levels = unique(level)))



# create long format 
md_forest_long <- md_forest_df %>% 
  filter(!term %in% c("(Intercept)", "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y", "d_MDtoBC_y", "d_md"),
         !is.na(level),
         ) %>% 
  select(level, OR_mdl6, conf.low_mdl6, conf.high_mdl6, OR_mdl8, conf.low_mdl8, conf.high_mdl8
         ) %>% 
  mutate(pct_diff = if_else(is.na(OR_mdl6), "-", as.character(round((OR_mdl8 - OR_mdl6) / OR_mdl8 * 100, 1))) 
         ) %>% 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(OR_mdl6, conf.low_mdl6, conf.high_mdl6, 
             OR_mdl8, conf.low_mdl8, conf.high_mdl8
             ),
    names_to = c(".value", "mdl"),
    names_sep = "_"
  ) 
is_summary <- is.na(md_forest_long$OR)


# develop forest

md_forest_long %>% 
  group_by(mdl
           ) %>% 
  mutate(mean = OR,
         lower = conf.low,
         upper = conf.high
         ) %>% 
  forestplot(labeltext = c(level, pct_diff),
             xlog = FALSE,
             xlab = "OR",
             boxsize = 0.3,
             zero = 1,
             line.margin = 0.5,
             clip = c(0, 3),
             ticks = c(0, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2, 2.5, 3),
             ci.vertices = T,
             ci.vertices.height = 0.2,
             legend = c("Default model", "Adjusted for MD"),
             title = paste("Final model adjusted vs unadjusted for continuous mamographic breast density"),
             is.summary = is_summary
             ) %>% 
  fp_set_style(box = c("grey50","darkorange") %>%  lapply(function(x) gpar(fill = x, col = "#666666")),
               default = gpar(vertices = T),
               line = "grey40",
               summary = gpar(col = NA), # Set summary color to NA
               align = "lrrr",
               hrz_lines = gpar(col = NA), # Set line color to NA
               txt_gp = fpTxtGp(label = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    ticks = gpar(cex = 0.8),
                                    xlab = gpar(cex = 0.8)
                                )
               ) %>% 
  fp_decorate_graph(graph.pos = 2
                    ) %>% 
  fp_add_header(level = c("", "Risk factor"),
                pct_diff = c("", "% difference")
                ) %>% 
  fp_insert_row(level = "",
                position = 3,
                mean = NULL,
                lower = NULL,
                upper = NULL,
                is.summary = TRUE,
                boxsize = NA)

6.3 ST 2 - TC descriptives by detection mode with logistic regression results

Similar to table 3 but with TC instead of risk factors

# summary Logistic Regression of Model 10  -----------------------------------------



# remove columns with total and interval N - not simple with gtsummary, trying from scratch without adding the columns


# Create clean version of logistic output, without Ns

model <- df %>%
  drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>%
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ 
      diagage + d_R1toBC_y
      + d_inv_status + d_grade_tr + d_morph4_tr_lab + d_pos_nodes_tr_lab 
      + d_tmsize_tr_lab + d_er_tr_lab + d_pr_tr_lab + d_her2_tr_lab, 
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# tidy up output
model10 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_inv_status ~ "Invasive status",
                          d_grade_tr ~ "Grade",
                          d_morph4_tr_lab ~ "Morphology type",
                          d_pos_nodes_tr_lab ~ "Positive nodes",
                          d_tmsize_tr_lab ~ "Tumour size",
                          d_er_tr_lab ~ "ER status",
                          d_pr_tr_lab ~ "PR status",
                          d_her2_tr_lab ~ "HER2 status"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")
  

#model10




# Create descriptives by mode of detection ------------------------------------
# select(d_dmode_n, d_R1alcohol_units_lab, d_R1smokingstatus_lab, d_bmi_entry_lab,  d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab, d_bbd_lab, d_fambrca_lab, d_age_menarche_lab, d_ocstatus_lab, d_R1menopause_lab3, d_age_meno_tr_lab, d_R1hrt_tr_lab, d_parity_tr_lab, d_agebirth1_tr_lab)

# note: need to rename the non-trick variables according to their trick name to align with the model, which can then allow table merge. 

# include missing 
# create df
  
descr_model10_df <- df %>% 
  select(d_dmode, all_of(tumour_char), diagage, d_R1toBC_y 
         ) %>% 
  mutate(d_grade_lab = as.character(if_else(d_grade_lab == "DCIS", NA, d_grade_lab)) # recode dcis to missing
  ) %>% 
  mutate(d_grade_lab = factor(x = d_grade_lab,
                              levels = c(1, 2, 3))
         ) %>% 
  mutate(d_grade_tr = d_grade_lab,
         d_morph4_tr_lab = d_morph4_lab,
         d_pos_nodes_tr_lab = d_pos_nodes_lab,
         d_tmsize_tr_lab = d_tumour_size_lab,
         d_er_tr_lab = d_er_status_lab,
         d_pr_tr_lab = d_pr_status_lab,
         d_her2_tr_lab = d_her2_status_lab
         ) %>% 
  select(-d_grade_lab, -d_morph4_lab, -d_pos_nodes_lab, -d_tumour_size_lab, -d_er_status_lab, -d_pr_status_lab, -d_her2_status_lab, -diagage, -d_R1toBC_y)  
  
  

# glimpse(descr_model2)
# str(test)

# create gs summary table
descr_model10 <- descr_model10_df %>% 
  tbl_summary(by = d_dmode,
              statistic = list(
                all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p})"
                ),
              digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
              label = list(
                          d_inv_status ~ "Invasive status",
                          d_grade_tr ~ "Grade",
                          d_morph4_tr_lab ~ "Morphology type",
                          d_pos_nodes_tr_lab ~ "Positive nodes",
                          d_tmsize_tr_lab ~ "Tumour size",
                          d_er_tr_lab ~ "ER status",
                          d_pr_tr_lab ~ "PR status",
                          d_her2_tr_lab ~ "HER2 status"
                         )
              ) %>% 
  add_overall()

#descr_model10
  
# merge with analysis output -------------------------------------------------


ST2 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(descr_model10, model10),
  tab_spanner = c("", "")
   ) 
   
  
   

ST2
Characteristic

# this ID is extra in the LR output from model 2 - T276E210
# this is because HRT status was missing as under 50 but it is present in trick hrt as recoded to never. - check what to do with this

6.4 ST 3 - Risk factor analysis adjusted for tumour characteristics (sensitivity analysis) - on subset of cases with TC

using model 3 (default model on subset of cases with tumour characteristics) and model 4 (adjusting for TC)

similar layout to table 4

# Model 3 - subset on those with TC----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

 model <- df %>%
   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y,
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

# tidy up output
model3 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")


model3a <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n(location = "level")  %>%
  add_nevent(location = "level") %>%
  add_global_p() %>%
  modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                stat_n = "**Total N**")



#model3
#model3a

# create a dataframe with broom::tidy() for forestplots 

tidy_model3 <- model %>%
   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
   select(-estimate) %>%
   select(term, OR, everything())
#tidy_model3


# Model 4 ----------------------------------------------------------------
 # include parity and meno trick variable instead of original variables

# only included alcohol status - check if we need to do the trick if including both
# including "diagage", "d_R1toBC_y" - MD variables will be included in sub analysis

model <- df %>%
   tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
           ) %>% 
  glm(formula = d_dmode_n ~ d_R1menopause_lab3 + d_age_meno_tr_lab 
      + d_R1hrt_tr_lab
      + d_parity_lab + d_agebirth1_tr_lab + d_age_menarche_lab + d_ocstatus_lab 
      + d_bbd_lab + d_fambrca_lab
      + d_R1alcohol_units_lab + d_R1smokingstatus_lab 
      + d_bmi_entry_lab + d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab
      + diagage + d_R1toBC_y
      + d_inv_status + d_grade_tr + d_morph4_tr_lab + d_pos_nodes_tr_lab 
      + d_tmsize_tr_lab + d_er_tr_lab + d_pr_tr_lab + d_her2_tr_lab, 
            family = binomial(link = "logit"))

model4 <- tbl_regression(model, 
                         exponentiate = TRUE,
                         label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth",
                          d_inv_status ~ "Invasive status",
                          d_grade_tr ~ "Grade",
                          d_morph4_tr_lab ~ "Morphology type",
                          d_pos_nodes_tr_lab ~ "Positive nodes",
                          d_tmsize_tr_lab ~ "Tumour size",
                          d_er_tr_lab ~ "ER status",
                          d_pr_tr_lab ~ "PR status",
                          d_her2_tr_lab ~ "HER2 status"
                         )
                         ) %>% 
  add_global_p() %>% 
  remove_row_type(variables = c(diagage, d_R1toBC_y), # remove covariates
                  type = "all")

# model4
# 
 model4a <- tbl_regression(model, exponentiate = TRUE) %>%
  add_n(location = "level")  %>%
   add_nevent(location = "level") %>%
   add_global_p() %>%
   modify_header(stat_nevent = "**Interval N**",
                 stat_n = "**Total N**")

# model4a
# model3a


# create a dataframe with broom::tidy() for forestplots 

tidy_model4 <- model %>%
   tidy(exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
   mutate(OR = estimate) %>%
   select(-estimate) %>%
   select(term, OR, everything())


 # Descriptives for model 4 -------------------------------------------------
# subset on those with mammo density 


descr_TCsubset_df <- df %>% 
  select(d_dmode,  all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables), all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(tumour_char), diagage, d_R1toBC_y) %>% 
  tidyr::drop_na(all_of(tumour_char_tr), all_of(all_expl_variables_tr), diagage, d_R1toBC_y
  ) %>% 
  mutate(d_bmi_entry_lab = fct_relevel(d_bmi_entry_lab, "<18.5", "18.5-25", "25-30", "30+")
              ) %>%
  # recode the names of normal variables to trick to allow merge between regression tables and this descriptive table
  mutate(d_age_meno_tr_lab = d_age_menopause_lab,
         d_R1hrt_tr_lab = d_R1hrtstatus_lab,
         d_agebirth1_tr_lab = d_agebirth1_lab,
         d_grade_tr = d_grade_lab,
         d_morph4_tr_lab = d_morph4_lab,
         d_pos_nodes_tr_lab = d_pos_nodes_lab,
         d_tmsize_tr_lab = d_tumour_size_lab,
         d_er_tr_lab = d_er_status_lab,
         d_pr_tr_lab = d_pr_status_lab,
         d_her2_tr_lab = d_her2_status_lab
         ) %>%
  select(-d_age_menopause_lab, -d_R1hrtstatus_lab, -d_agebirth1_lab, -diagage, -d_R1toBC_y, -d_grade_lab, -d_morph4_lab, -d_pos_nodes_lab, -d_tumour_size_lab, -d_er_status_lab, -d_pr_status_lab, -d_her2_status_lab)
  
  

# glimpse(descr_MDsubset_df)
# str(descr_MDsubset_df)

# create gs summary table
descr_TCsubset <- descr_TCsubset_df %>% 
  tbl_summary(by = d_dmode,
              statistic = list(
                all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})",
                all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p})"
                ),
              digits = all_continuous() ~ 2,
              label = list(
                          d_R1alcohol_units_lab ~ "Alcohol units",
                          d_R1smokingstatus_lab ~ "Smoking status",
                          d_bmi_entry_lab ~ "BMI",
                          d_R1physmet_leis_who_lab ~ "Physical Activity (WHO cat)",
                          d_bbd_lab ~ "Benign breast disease",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "Family history of BC",
                          d_age_menarche_lab ~ "Age at menarche",
                          d_ocstatus_lab ~ "Oral contraceptives status",
                          d_R1menopause_lab3 ~ "Menopause",
                          d_age_meno_tr_lab ~ "Age at menopause",
                          d_R1hrt_tr_lab ~ "HRT status",
                          d_parity_lab ~ "N of parous pregnancies",
                          d_agebirth1_tr_lab ~ "Age at first birth",
                          d_inv_status ~ "Invasive status",
                          d_grade_tr ~ "Grade",
                          d_morph4_tr_lab ~ "Morphology type",
                          d_pos_nodes_tr_lab ~ "Positive nodes",
                          d_tmsize_tr_lab ~ "Tumour size",
                          d_er_tr_lab ~ "ER status",
                          d_pr_tr_lab ~ "PR status",
                          d_her2_tr_lab ~ "HER2 status"
                ),
              type = list(d_bbd_lab ~ "categorical",
                          d_fambrca_lab ~ "categorical")
              ) #%>% 
  #add_overall()


#descr_TCsubset
# check numbers against
# model4a
# model3a
 


# merge tables -------------------------------------------------


ST3 <- tbl_merge(
  tbls = list(descr_TCsubset, model3, model4),
  tab_spanner = c("Subset with TC" ,"Unadjusted for TC", "Adjusted for TC")
   ) 
   

#ST3


# adding pct difference -------------------------------------------

#  using gtsummary 
ST3_diff <- ST3 %>% 
  modify_table_body(
    ~ .x %>% 
      mutate(pct_diff = if_else(
          is.na(estimate_2) | is.na(estimate_3),
          "",
          sprintf("%.1f", (estimate_3 - estimate_2) / estimate_3 * 100))
    )
    ) %>% 
  modify_header(
    pct_diff = "**Difference (%)**"
  )

ST3_diff
Characteristic Subset with TC Unadjusted for TC Adjusted for TC Difference (%)
I, N = 6741 SD, N = 1,0331 OR2 95% CI2 p-value OR2 95% CI2 p-value
Menopause



0.4

>0.9
    Premenopausal 209 (31) 310 (30) — —
— —
    Postmenopausal 465 (69) 723 (70) 0.84 0.55, 1.27
0.97 0.61, 1.54
13.6
Age at menopause



0.026

0.064
